ARTICLE_TITLE: Global Health Authorities Track Cruise Ship Passengers After Hantavirus Outbreak
ARTICLE_BODY: Health authorities worldwide are tracking passengers from a cruise ship following a hantavirus outbreak on board. The incident has prompted international health agencies to launch a coordinated response to prevent further spread of the disease. Hantavirus is a serious respiratory illness that can be transmitted through contact with infected rodents or their droppings. The outbreak on the cruise vessel has raised concerns about passenger safety and disease control measures on ships. Health officials are working to identify and contact all passengers who may have been exposed to the virus during their voyage. This includes passengers who have already disembarked at various ports around the world. The international cooperation demonstrates how quickly health authorities can respond to potential pandemic threats in our interconnected world. Passengers are being advised to monitor their health closely and seek medical attention if they develop symptoms such as fever, muscle aches, or breathing difficulties.
W1EX: Health authorities are tracking the spread of the disease.
W2EX: The incident occurred on a cruise ship last week.
W4EX: The outbreak has affected several passengers.
W5EX: Passengers disembarked at different ports worldwide.
P1EX: Governments will launch a coordinated response to the crisis.
P2EX: Officials need to identify and contact all exposed individuals.
P3EX: Patients should monitor their health closely for symptoms.

Q6: Hantavirus can only be transmitted between humans.
EXP6: Hantavirus is transmitted through contact with infected rodents or their droppings, not primarily between humans.

EXP7: The article states that health authorities worldwide are coordinating to track passengers who may have been exposed.
